avoid.market: Will the Fed cut rates 0 times? (KXRATECUTCOUNT-26DEC31-T0)
avoid.reason: Deep, liquid market (1.9M volume) with tight spread. The 0-cuts market (0.802) plus the at-least-one-cut market (0.2265) sums to ~1.03, only a ~3pt overround across two contracts with slightly different resolution windows — insufficient edge vs the 8% macro threshold after spreads. No independent post-cutoff FOMC data to justify a directional divergence from market consensus.

avoid.market: Will the Federal Reserve cut rates before 2027? (KXRATECUT-26DEC31)
avoid.reason: Same complement structure as the 0-cuts market; no credible internal-knowledge basis for a 2026 Fed path forecast that beats a highly liquid market by 8%+.

Passes volume/spread filters, but resolution is hours away and depends entirely on the live September 2026 WTI contract price, which I cannot observe. Brent strike ladder implies spot Brent ~$85-86 and a normal ~$6-7 Brent-WTI spread, making the 0.845 pricing internally consistent. No edge without live spot data.

avoid.market: Will there be a recession in 2027? (KXRECSSNBER-27)
avoid.reason: Resolution window (Q4 2026-Q4 2027) is far beyond any grounded forecasting horizon. 0.405 implied is within the plausible range given elevated inflation and a hawkish Fed, but I have no specific data source to justify an 8%+ divergence. Low 24h volume also suggests stale pricing.
All strikes below the $50K volume floor except none qualify; ladder is monotonic and internally consistent (distribution centered ~3.3-3.4%). No post-cutoff CPI print data to fade the market.
All strikes below the $50K volume filter. Additionally, no access to current GDPNow/consensus tracking estimates for Q2 2026, so no credible grounding source.
All fail the $50K volume filter; many have zero volume and wide spreads. Ladder pricing is internally consistent around gold ~$4,000 and silver ~$56 spot, which I cannot independently verify.

All fail the $50K volume filter. Minor ladder inconsistencies exist (e.g., EPACMAJ >6 at 0.42 vs >7 at 0.405) but positions are untradeable at this depth and I lack current-season ACE/storm-count data.
Fail the $50K volume filter and show zero 24h volume. No access to current NWS point forecasts; same-day temperature markets without a forecast source are pure gambling.
Fail volume filter; intraday crypto band markets require live spot and vol data, unavailable. Crypto threshold of 12% unreachable without it.

notes: Regime classified as inflationary: markets price ~80% probability of zero Fed cuts in 2026, CPI YoY distribution centered around 3.3-3.4%, gold near $4,000/oz, silver mid-$50s, and Brent in the mid-$80s — a stagflation-tilted backdrop with real GDP expectations clustered near 1.5-2.0%. Only four markets pass the liquidity/spread filters (both Fed-cut contracts, same-day WTI, and 2027 recession). The Fed pair shows only a ~3pt combined overround — below the 8% macro edge threshold after transaction costs. The remaining candidates require live spot prices or post-knowledge-cutoff macro data I cannot verify, and fabricating grounding sources is prohibited. Zero trades is the disciplined output today. Watch items: any FOMC communication shift would reprice the rate-cut complex; the Jul 30 Q2 GDP advance estimate and Aug 12 CPI release are the next major catalysts if liquidity improves in those ladders.
